rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Motion To Enforce Stay Of Matters Embraced In The Appeals Of Orders Denying Motions To Vacate July 29 2019 Order & Ccsf Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANTS NOTICE OF MOTION TO ENFORCE STAY OF MATTERS EMBRACED IN THE APPEALS OF ORDERS DENYING MOTIONS TO VACATE JULY 29, 2019 ORDER & CCSF INJUNCTION is continued to July 30, 2021 to be heard with other pending motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARIA LUBARSKY VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY A CALAIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the cause of action for tenant harassment is sustained without leave to amend, barred by the statute of limitations; demurrer to the cause of action for elder abuse is sustained without leave to amend, Plaintiff fails to provide any authority for the proposition that this cause of action is available under the circumstances of this case. Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of Unruh Act is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action; this cause of action is not presently sufficiently alleged and is uncertain due to what appears to be mistakenly included allegations. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEWIN PROPERTIES, INC. VS. LORENA HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Application To Order Clerk To Perform Ministerial Act Of Filing Notice Of Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 28, 2021 line 6. Defendant's Application To Order Clerk To Perform Ministerial Act Of Filing Notice Of Appeal Plaintiff shall file its Notice of Appeal in this limited jurisdiction case for the Appellate Panel. This Court will not decide whether Appellate Panel has jurisdiction. This decision shall be made by the Appellate Panel. See Citibank, N.A., v. Tabalon (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th Supp, 16 cited by the moving party. This Court will note that Defendant did not cite any authority for the proposition that denials of ANTI-SLAPP motions in limited jurisdiction cases are appealable given that ANTI-SLAPP motions are not allowed in limited jurisdiction cases. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
